static_findings.trigger_condition: Execution reaches free(buf) twice without reset or reallocation.
evidence: Pointer 'buf' is first freed at source line 8.
Pointer 'buf' is freed again at source line 10: free(buf);
static_findings.suggested_fix: Set buf to NULL after free and avoid duplicate ownership.

static_findings.trigger_condition: Execution reaches free(tmp) twice without reset or reallocation.
evidence: Pointer 'tmp' is first freed at source line 9.
Pointer 'tmp' is freed again at source line 13: free(tmp);
static_findings.suggested_fix: Set tmp to NULL after free and avoid duplicate ownership.

static_findings.trigger_condition: Execution reaches free(tmp) twice without reset or reallocation.
evidence: Pointer 'tmp' is first freed at source line 9.
Pointer 'tmp' is freed again at source line 17: free(tmp);
static_findings.suggested_fix: Set tmp to NULL after free and avoid duplicate ownership.

static_findings.trigger_condition: Attacker-controlled input may be interpreted as a format string, enabling memory disclosure or arbitrary writes via %x/%s/%n tokens.
evidence: Function 'fprintf' uses a non-literal format argument at source line 3: fprintf(out, event);
Format argument expression: event
static_findings.suggested_fix: Use a constant format string such as printf("%s", input), fprintf(stream, "%s", input), or snprintf(buf, size, "%s", input).

static_findings.trigger_condition: Attacker-controlled input may be interpreted as a format string, enabling memory disclosure or arbitrary writes via %x/%s/%n tokens.
evidence: Function 'printf' uses a non-literal format argument at source line 3: printf(msg);
Format argument expression: msg
static_findings.suggested_fix: Use a constant format string such as printf("%s", input), fprintf(stream, "%s", input), or snprintf(buf, size, "%s", input).

static_findings.trigger_condition: Oversized or attacker-controlled input may reach a copy operation without sufficient length validation.
evidence: Dangerous copy-style function call at source line 11: memcpy(buf, msg, len);
Rule fallback cannot fully prove destination bounds.
static_findings.suggested_fix: Validate length before copying and use bounded APIs with explicit destination size.

static_findings.trigger_condition: Pointer 'score' is dereferenced or accessed after free.
evidence: Pointer 'score' is freed at source line 10.
Pointer 'score' is used again at source line 13: printf("score=%d\n", *score);
static_findings.suggested_fix: Do not access score after free; set it to NULL and guard future uses.
